chapters.name: Some Basic Concepts in Chemistry
concepts: Concept of atom, molecule, element and compound

chapters.name: Atomic Structure
concepts: Bohr model of a hydrogen atom - its postulates

Concept of atomic orbitals as one-electron wave functions

Various quantum numbers (n, l, and ml, ms)
Shapes of s, p and d orbitals

Extra stability of half-filled and completely filled orbitals

chapters.name: Chemical Bonding and molecular Structure
concepts: Kossel-Lewis approach to chemical bond formation
concept of ionic and covalent bonds
Factors affecting the formation of ionic bonds
Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR) theory and shapes of simple molecules

Concept of bond order, bond length and bond energy

chapters.name: Chemical Thermodynamics
concepts: Laws of thermodynamics (zeroth, first, second, third) and their implications

Heat capacity and molar heat capacity
Hess's law of constant heat summation
Enthalpies of bond dissociation, combustion, formation, atomization, sublimation, phase transition, hydration, ionization and solution
ΔS of the universe and ΔG of the system as criterion for spontaneity

chapters.name: Solutions
concepts: Different methods for expressing the concentration of solution
Plots for ideal and non-ideal solutions
Van't Hoff factor and its significance

chapters.name: Equilibrium
concepts: Characteristics of equilibrium involving physical processes

chapters.name: Redox Reactions
concepts: Electronic concepts of oxidation and reduction
Molar conductivities and their variation with concentration

concepts: factors affecting the rate of reactions: concentration, temperature, pressureand catalyst

chapters.name: Classification of elements and Periodicity in properties
concepts: Modern periodic law and present form of the periodic table

Atomic radii (trend across periods and groups)
Chemical reactivity trends across periods and groups

concepts: General trends in physical properties across periods
General trends in chemical properties across periods

concepts: General trends in the first-row transition elements
Preparation, properties and uses of K2Cr2O7
